overview.description: Establish a 2,400-acre industrial zone targeting advanced manufacturing, projected to create 25,000 jobs with export-oriented incentives.

agentSequence.label: Economic Agent
findings: 25,000 direct jobs projected; ₹1.8B annual tax revenue at maturity
Export multiplier effect: 2.3x regional GDP contribution
SME supplier ecosystem could add 8,000 indirect jobs

agentSequence.label: Social Agent
findings: Adjacent villages face displacement pressure — 3,200 households affected
Skill training programs critical for local employment share
Public acceptance split along income lines

agentSequence.label: Environmental Agent
findings: Water table stress in semi-arid zone — mitigation required
Emissions increase +22% without green manufacturing standards
Wetland buffer zone protection mandatory

agentSequence.label: Stakeholder Agent
findings: Businesses and government strongly support (88% / 79%)
Environmental advocates oppose (62% opposition)
Local residents divided on employment vs. displacement

agentSequence.label: Risk Agent
findings: Environmental clearance timeline risk: high
Land acquisition litigation probability: medium-high
Water rights disputes possible in drought years

agentSequence.label: Future Shock Agent
findings: Industrial zone → Job growth → Migration influx → Housing pressure
Supply chain shock could accelerate domestic manufacturing +20%
Climate regulation may require retrofit costs by 2035

agentSequence.label: Chief Decision Officer
findings: Viability score: 61/100 — proceed with strict environmental conditions
Recommend green manufacturing standards as binding requirement
Phased land release tied to compliance milestones

consequences.description: Traffic Reduction leads to Property Value Growth

Relocated closer to city center for better mobility and career growth.

recommendation.alternativeScenario: Reduce zone to 1,200 acres with stricter environmental buffer
recommendation.executiveSummary: New Industrial Zone offers substantial economic upside but carries elevated environmental and social risk. Conditional approval with green standards recommended.
